text.title: Seven Performance Domains (PMBoK 8) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the seven-performance-domains methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Always use exactly these seven domain names — never add an 8th, never reintroduce 'Integration Management', 'Quality', or 'Communications' as standalone domains.
rule.rationale: PMBoK 8 explicitly integrates Quality, Communications, and Procurement; LLMs trained on PMBoK 6 hallucinate these as standalone domains.

rule.statement: Walk the seven domains in fixed order (Governance, Scope, Schedule, Finance, Stakeholders, Resources, Risk) and emit one record per domain.
rule.rationale: Fixed order makes diffs across weekly runs stable; reordered output adds noise to version control.

rule.statement: Run the domain audit before every steering committee; the output IS the status pack, not a separate manual summarisation.
rule.rationale: Manual summarisation introduces optimism bias and lag.

rule.statement: Map each missing domain artefact directly to a risk entry in the Risk domain register.
rule.rationale: Risk registers that do not include process gaps undercount project risk systematically.

rule.statement: When a process feels homeless, default to Governance; do not invent a new domain.
rule.rationale: Inventing domains breaks the canonical seven and bloats taxonomy.

list.item: Pure-Scrum teams where Scrum events already cover domains implicitly
Solopreneur projects under 1 week with a single deliverable
Shops still on PMBoK 6 in mid-project — switching narrative confuses sponsors
